title: What to Know About the Supreme Court’s Birthright Citizenship Order
summary: The decision ends one of the most aggressive parts of Trump’s immigration agenda. But hundreds of other restrictions have taken effect.

title: Supreme Court to Weigh Constitutional Protection for AR-15 Rifles
summary: Two cases to be heard in the fall challenge legality of state and local bans on the weapons, which are popular with gun owners and have been used in mass shootings.

title: After U.S.-Iran War, Oman Is Said to Propose Strait of Hormuz Fee Plan
summary: Under the proposal, Iran and Oman would collect payment for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, according to officials and diplomats with knowledge of the matter.

title: Shipping Traffic in Strait of Hormuz Picks Up Again
summary: Vessels stranded for months have started moving in larger numbers, but many pulled back over the weekend after Iran and the U.S. exchanged attacks.

title: Tom Kean, New Jersey Congressman, Says Depression Led to Long Absence
summary: After months of mystery, the New Jersey representative broke his silence about the undisclosed health issue that prompted his 117-day absence.

title: The Founding Father Who Sought a Last-Minute Deal to Avert the Revolution
summary: Recently discovered documents reveal a back-channel attempt by a leader of the Continental Congress to make peace. (He did not succeed.)

title: Record-Breaking Heat Wave Expected to Hit New York City Ahead of July 4 Weekend
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani signed an executive order that aims to protect workers who must toil outside. Temperatures this week could exceed 100 degrees.

title: Fourth of July Heat Wave Forecast: Here Are the Day-by-Day Temperatures
summary: A day-by-day breakdown of where dangerous heat is expected through the Fourth of July weekend.

title: Ontario and Quebec Face Heat Warnings as High Temperatures Spread Across Canada
summary: Parts of Ontario and Quebec will be well above normal temperatures this week as a heat wave spreads over North America.

title: Bending Spoons, Owner of AOL and Other Old Internet Brands, Is Going Public
summary: Bending Spoons, an Italian company that buys aging internet companies, is going public this week at a potential value of $19 billion.

title: How Grindr’s C.E.O. Adopted A.I.: ‘I Just Imposed It’
summary: George Arison, the gay dating app’s chief executive, is aiming for all code to be eventually written by artificial intelligence, making the company “leaner.”

title: Mamdani and City Council Agree on $126 Billion Budget
summary: A handshake deal, reached right at the statutory budget deadline, will expand programs that offer housing vouchers and discounted transit fares.

title: The Prom Went On in Kyiv, but Masha’s Date Danced Alone
summary: Masha Polska, 15, was an avid dancer who had been dreaming of a star turn in the group waltz. That was not to be.

title: Comcast, NBCUniversal Split Drives Deal-Making Speculation
summary: Almost as soon as the two companies announced a split, analysts rushed to consider potential merger and acquisition targets for each.

title: On the ‘Lux Tour’, Rosalía Shape Shifts Through Dance
summary: On the “Lux” tour, the pop star mingles dance genres, starting the show on pointe. As with her dip into opera, her technique might not be perfect, but the intention is good.

title: The Supreme Court Gets Birthright Citizenship Right
summary: The Constitution is clear. People who are born in this country and subject to its laws are citizens.

title: I’m Gay, Not Queer. It Matters.
summary: Polyamorous? Queer. Vaguely uncomfortable with gender expectations? Possibly queer, too.

title: With New Seizures, Value of the Met’s Looted Artifacts Tops $95 Million
summary: The Manhattan district attorney’s office said it secured the return of dozens of ancient artifacts in June, after a process that the museum described as collaborative.
